# Requirement: FR-009

- Title: Deliver multilingual site experience for English, Dutch, and Portuguese
- Status: Active

## Requirement Statement

The system shall allow visitors to view the full top-level marketing site in English, Dutch, and Portuguese, and switch between those languages without losing their current page context.

## Rationale

The site represents a consultant working across international and Dutch-speaking audiences. Offering English, Dutch, and Portuguese improves accessibility, trust, and relevance for visitors who are more likely to engage in their preferred language.

## Acceptance Conditions

- A language switcher is visible in the shared site header and available on all top-level pages.
- `Home`, `Projects`, `About Me`, and `Contact` are available in `en`, `nl`, and `pt`.
- Switching language keeps the visitor on the equivalent page when that translation exists.
- English remains the default public fallback and existing primary routes are preserved unless explicitly approved otherwise.
- Page titles, descriptions, and social/SEO metadata are localized for each supported language.
- No page ships with accidental mixed-language UI copy, except intentional proper nouns or brand names.

## Success Metrics

- Visitors can reach the main site content in all three supported languages with consistent navigation behavior.
- Build and route generation stay green after multilingual support is introduced.
- The localized experience improves message clarity for Dutch-speaking and Portuguese-speaking visitors.

## Non-Goals

- Runtime machine translation.
- CMS/editor translation workflow in this phase.
- Additional languages beyond English, Dutch, and Portuguese.

## Constraints

- Preserve the static-first Astro architecture and repository-local content model.
- Prefer shared dictionaries/content structures over duplicated component logic.
- Avoid breaking the current English route contract without explicit approval.
